rationale: The citing paper discusses Pauwels, Hanssens, and Siddarth (2002) in its core elasticity-results section, reporting that paper's specific decomposition findings (small brand-switching shares for storable and perishable products). It uses those results to benchmark and interpret its own decomposition outcomes, positioning its estimates as more consistent with recent evidence than with older high-switching estimates. The authors also explicitly note methodological differences (dynamic time-series versus their model), indicating this is an interpretive comparison rather than a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited paper as a substantive empirical benchmark when interpreting its own incidence-choice-quantity elasticity decomposition results. It aligns its findings with Pauwels et al.'s lower brand-switching shares while noting methodological differences in modeling dynamic effects.
